rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SHIGEO KUBOTA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement Between All Heirs To Decedent Shigeo Kubota
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The court to determine how to proceed. 2) Objector filed a Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement and requests a. The 2016 Grant Deed shall be rescinded. b. Pursuant to 850(a)(3)(B) and 170000 the 40th Avenue property shall be confirmed as an asset of the Kubota Family 199 Trust. A new Grant Deed shall issue transferring the 40th Ave Property from Shigeo Kubota, widower to Shigeyuki Kubota, Trustee of the Kubota 1999 Trust. c. Trustee shall transfer the 100% interest to Shigeyuki Kubota, Haruyuki Kubota and Aliso Kubota. d. The parties agree to be bound by the Co-Tenancy Agreement attached as Exhibit A. e. Pursuant to 850(a)(3)(B) and 17000 Bank of America checking account x4344 smf savings account x5482 shall be confirmed an asset of the Kubota 1999 Trust. f. Aliso shall dismiss her petition for probate and the dismissal extends to Shigeyuki's creditor claims. g. Shigeyuki shall have sole and absolute right to Shigeo's tangible personal property.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PATRICIA ROSEMARY ALLEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/7/2021. 1) A creditor's claim of the personal representatives or the attorney for over $2,500 will not be approved by the Court until either a hearing has been held or written consent of the beneficiaries is on file. Such hearing must be held as set forth in Probate Code 9252(a) and notice thereof given to all persons entitled thereto including all residuary beneficiaries, together with a copy of the claim. [LRSF 14.29]. 2) Please give notice to the Canadian Consulate. [Probate Code 1202]. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/24/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALEKSANDER ALEKSANDROVICH DONSKOY AKA ALEKSANDER ALEKSANDROVICH DONSKOY AKA DONSKOI ALEKSANDER ALEKSAND
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/9/2021. 1) Each document in response to the Examiner notes must be filed separately and not as part of a Declaration. Notice DE-121 must be filed separately. Proof of publication must be filed separately. Duties and Liabilities must be filed separately. Declarations of Donskaya Anatalievna must be filed separately. Confidential statement must be filed separately see issue #2. 2) Attorney John Saadeh has filed the Confidential Statement several times as part of other documents that are not confidential. Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] 3) Must prove the Will is valid pursuant to California law or prove valid under the laws of the place where it was executed (Probate Code 12520 et seq). 4) Examiner has not received the required courtesy copy of any documents filed by Objector and any documents field by Petitioner after 4/19/2021. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 5) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/24/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHUEN MOON LAU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/26/2021 1) A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] In response Attorney Sarah B. Sheppard filed a Declaration. The Declaration must be signed and verified by Petitioner. (CRC 7.103). An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/10/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/17/2021. 1) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.
